questions.question: A PyTorch tensor differs from a NumPy array because:
options: It can run on GPU and tracks gradients automatically via autograd
questions.explanation: Tensors are the foundation: GPU-capable + differentiable.

questions.question: requires_grad=True in PyTorch:
options: Tells autograd to track operations on the tensor so gradients can be computed

questions.question: The five steps in a PyTorch training iteration:
questions.explanation: Forgetting zero_grad() accumulates gradients across batches -- a common bug.

questions.question: nn.Module is the base class for:
options: All neural network components -- layers, models, loss functions
questions.explanation: Subclass nn.Module to define custom models with trainable parameters.

questions.question: model.eval() vs model.train():
options: eval() disables dropout and uses running stats for batchnorm; train() enables them
questions.explanation: Always call model.eval() before inference and model.train() before training epochs.

questions.question: torch.no_grad() is used during:
options: Inference and evaluation -- disables gradient computation to save memory and speed up computation
questions.explanation: with torch.no_grad(): reduces memory usage by not storing intermediate gradients.

questions.question: A DataLoader provides:
options: Batched, shuffled, parallel data loading with pin_memory and num_workers options
questions.explanation: DataLoader handles the full data pipeline from dataset to batches.

questions.question: The Adam optimizer combines:
options: Momentum (exponential moving average of gradients) and RMSProp (adaptive learning rates)
questions.explanation: Adam adapts learning rates per parameter and uses momentum. Usually the best default choice.

questions.question: torch.save() and torch.load() are used for:
options: Saving and loading model weights (state_dict) or entire checkpoint dictionaries
questions.explanation: torch.save(model.state_dict(), 'model.pt') is the standard checkpoint pattern.

questions.question: model.to(device) moves:
options: All model parameters and buffers to the specified device (CPU or CUDA GPU)
questions.explanation: Must also move input tensors to device: X = X.to(device) before forward pass.
